questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "She painted the whole apartment ___"
questions.answer: herself — nobody helped her at all.
ourselves at the end-of-year party.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "Be careful with that knife — ___"
questions.answer: you might cut yourself.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "He taught himself to play guitar ___"
questions.answer: because there was no teacher nearby.
themselves sandwiches after school.

questions.prompt: Marco burned _____ when he was cooking dinner last night.
questions.answer: himself — The subject is 'he' (Marco), so the reflexive pronoun is himself.

questions.prompt: We introduced _____ to the new students on the first day of term.
questions.answer: ourselves — The subject is 'we', so the reflexive pronoun is ourselves.

questions.prompt: Did you make this whole cake _____, or did someone help you?
questions.answer: yourself — 'Yourself' emphasises that the subject did it alone. Subject = you (singular) → yourself.

questions.prompt: The little girl dressed _____ without any help from her parents.
questions.answer: herself — The subject is 'she' (the girl), so the reflexive pronoun is herself.

questions.prompt: I couldn't believe it — I had locked _____ out of the house!
questions.answer: myself — The subject is 'I', so the reflexive pronoun is myself.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "My brother fixed the car hisself last weekend."
questions.answer: There is no word 'hisself'. The correct reflexive pronoun for 'he' is himself.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "We enjoyed ourself at the school carnival last Friday."
questions.answer: The subject is 'we', so the reflexive pronoun must be ourselves, not ourself.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She made a cup of tea for hisself after the long meeting."
questions.answer: The subject is 'she', so the reflexive pronoun must be herself, not hisself.

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "Ourselves cleaned the classroom before the teacher arrived."
questions.answer: Reflexive pronouns cannot be the subject of a sentence. It should be: 'We cleaned the classroom ourselves…' or 'We cleaned the classroom before the teacher arrived.'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "They built the whole stage theirself before the show."
questions.answer: The plural reflexive pronoun for 'they' is themselves, not theirself.
She decorated the whole apartment herself.
Don't hurt yourself with those scissors!
We introduced ourselves to the new neighbours.
She left the house and locked herself out.
Did they build the whole stage themselves?
